label: 2021 La Vuelta ciclista a Espana Stage 3

extraction.one_line_summary: TAARAMÄE won from an 8-rider breakaway on Picon Blanco after ROGLIČ's Jumbo-Visma refused to chase, taking both stage and race lead.
tactical_patterns: ROGLIČ team refused to defend race lead against dangerous 8-rider break, revealing Jumbo-Visma prioritizes conserving energy over controlling early mountain stages.
BAYER from breakaway took KOM points on penultimate climb despite not contesting stage win, exposing jersey-hunting strategy within cooperative breaks.
CARAPAZ cracked on final climb despite being pre-race favorite, suggesting Olympics took significant toll on form just weeks later.
CARTHY lost additional time after Stage 2 crash positioning issues persisted, indicating cumulative time loss pattern from early-race incidents.

rider_signals.TAARAMÄE Rein: positive: At 34 years old, dominated experienced climbers DOMBROWSKI and ELISSONDE on 18% gradients, suggesting exceptional current form.
rider_signals.CARAPAZ Richard: negative: Struggled badly on Picon Blanco while teammate YATES looked strong, confirming post-Olympic fatigue concerns for GC ambitions.
rider_signals.YATES Adam: positive: Looked strong on final climb despite Stage 2 crash time loss, indicating fitness intact but race position compromised.
rider_signals.MAS Enric: positive: Finished strongly with LÓPEZ and ROGLIČ among favorites, signaling GC form despite conservative team tactics.
rider_signals.CARTHY Hugh: negative: Lost more time on Stage 3 after Stage 2 crash, establishing pattern of inability to recover position quickly.
rider_signals.BERNAL Egan: neutral: Took young rider jersey by positioning, suggesting steady GC pace without standout climbing performance.
rider_signals.DOMBROWSKI Joe: positive: Second place finish 34-year-old TAARAMÄE confirms maintained climbing ability on steep gradients at altitude.
